gaps.gap: Existing work studies iterative search refinement, but rarely measures the quality of final researcher approval decisions.
gaps.significance: Without direct quality measurement, it is hard to compare approval-loop systems fairly.
open_questions: How should final approval quality be operationalized?
Which decision-support signals improve approval quality most?

gaps.gap: Few studies compare approval-loop search systems under different levels of researcher feedback granularity.
gaps.significance: Feedback granularity likely changes both iteration speed and the quality of the accepted paper set.
open_questions: Does fine-grained feedback increase cognitive overhead?
What granularity best balances latency and precision?

hypotheses.hypothesis: Providing structured approval criteria improves final paper set quality.
hypotheses.rationale: Explicit decision criteria should reduce approval inconsistency and improve selection quality across rounds.
testability_notes: Define paper set quality with relevance and completeness metrics.
Keep baseline review conditions constant across groups.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Fine-grained researcher feedback reduces approval latency compared with coarse feedback.
hypotheses.rationale: More specific feedback should shorten revision cycles by making system adjustments easier to interpret.
testability_notes: Define latency as time from candidate set presentation to researcher approval.
Measure whether finer feedback increases annotation burden.

researcher_feedback.comment: Approved to move into experiment design.
